constructs: intent perception, motivation assignment, behavioral persistence
claim: Detecting intent in harmful acts catalyzes goal-directed motivation to blame that manifests in sustained effort to assign punishment, independent of actual harm magnitude.

constructs: payoff structure, cooperation incentive, self-interest rationality
claim: Self-interested third parties increase cooperation among disputants by altering payoff structure, yet systematically underestimate their positive impact and fail to intervene despite mutual benefit.

constructs: postural expansion, perceived dominance, partner selection
claim: Expansive body posture signals dominance and openness, increasing romantic attraction in modern rapid-decision dating contexts where kinetic cues are processed faster than personality information.

constructs: income level, structural inequality, entitlement sense, resource sharing
claim: High structural inequality fosters entitlement among higher-income individuals, reducing their generosity, while low inequality allows income to correlate with greater giving capacity and willingness to share.

phenomenon: racial bias in pain and treatment
constructs: false biological beliefs, pain rating, treatment accuracy, medical training
claim: Endorsement of false beliefs about racial biological differences predicts lower pain estimates for black versus white patients and less accurate treatment recommendations among medical trainees.

constructs: information withholding, volitional hiding, trustworthiness judgment
claim: Choosing not to disclose personal information signals untrustworthiness and leads to harsher judgment than admitting unsavory truth, yet decision-makers focus on damage of revealing rather than trust benefits of disclosing.
